title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Go Client Systems · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for header fields, media types, negotiation, json, and runtime validation, producing representation negotiation and validation boundary. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http representation negotiation and validation boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use Content-Type for enclosed content and Accept for preferred response media ranges with explicit fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

For Go Record what required effort · Review Language Charset Negotiation, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: JSON, text, bytes, Unicode code points, and user-perceived characters are interchangeable. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.
